Finding.description: One piece of evidence, grounded in the rendered input facts.
Finding.title: Finding

json_schema.description: Shared response shape for the source investigators.
json_schema.title: InvestigatorResponse

messages.content:
INCIDENT WINDOW
start: 2026-06-01T14:05:00+00:00
end: ongoing
rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-01T14:35:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

TRACE SPANS (grouped by trace, chronological)
trace trace_pre_01
  s1 booking-service POST /book start=2026-06-01T14:08:12+00:00 duration_ms=430 status=ok parent=-
  s2 payment-service check_eligibility start=2026-06-01T14:08:12+00:00 duration_ms=95 status=ok parent=s1
  s3 appointments-db eligibility_query start=2026-06-01T14:08:12+00:00 duration_ms=40 status=ok parent=s2
  s4 appointments-db reserve_slot start=2026-06-01T14:08:12+00:00 duration_ms=38 status=ok parent=s1
trace trace_incident_01
  s1 booking-service POST /book start=2026-06-01T14:39:05+00:00 duration_ms=3180 status=error parent=-
  s2 payment-service check_eligibility start=2026-06-01T14:39:05+00:00 duration_ms=2410 status=error parent=s1
  s3 appointments-db eligibility_query start=2026-06-01T14:39:05+00:00 duration_ms=2150 status=error parent=s2
  s4 appointments-db reserve_slot start=2026-06-01T14:39:08+00:00 duration_ms=310 status=ok parent=s1
trace trace_incident_02
  s1 booking-service POST /book start=2026-06-01T14:52:31+00:00 duration_ms=3320 status=error parent=-
  s2 payment-service check_eligibility start=2026-06-01T14:52:31+00:00 duration_ms=2480 status=error parent=s1
  s3 appointments-db eligibility_query start=2026-06-01T14:52:31+00:00 duration_ms=2200 status=error parent=s2
  s4 appointments-db reserve_slot start=2026-06-01T14:52:34+00:00 duration_ms=290 status=ok parent=s1

request.system:
You are one specialist in an evidence-first incident investigation pipeline
for a SaaS engineering team. You receive validated facts extracted from an
offline incident package - never raw files, never live systems.

Non-negotiable rules:
- Cite only information present in the input. Never invent timestamps,
  services, signals, or values.
- Copy timestamps exactly as they appear in the input (ISO-8601).
- Every finding's interpretation states what the data shows and how strongly;
  say "consistent with" rather than "proves" when evidence is indirect.
- You investigate and describe. You never recommend executing actions.
- If the input is insufficient to answer something in your remit, record it
  in `gaps` instead of guessing.
- Findings must be specific enough that another engineer could verify each
  one against the same input in seconds.

Role: trace investigator.
Input: distributed trace spans (grouped by trace) and the incident window.

Report as findings, each citing trace ids, spans, and durations:
- where time is spent in slow requests: which span dominates the critical
  path, and what fraction of the root duration it accounts for
- error propagation: which spans fail, and whether failures originate deep
  and surface upward
- before/after contrast when both healthy and degraded traces exist
  (same operations, changed durations)
- operations that stay fast even in degraded traces - they narrow the fault
Use duration numbers from the input; never estimate.

response.text: {"findings":[{"interpretation":"In the pre-incident trace, root span POST /book completes in 430ms with all child spans fast: check_eligibility 95ms (22.1% of root), eligibility_query 40ms (9.3% of root), reserve_slot 38ms (8.8% of root). This is the healthy baseline.","timestamp":"2026-06-01T14:08:12+00:00","service":"booking-service","signal":"root_span_duration_ms","value":430},{"interpretation":"In trace_incident_01, appointments-db eligibility_query (s3) is the dominant contributor to the critical path: 2150ms out of the 3180ms root duration (67.6% of total request time) and 89.2% of its parent check_eligibility span's 2410ms duration.","timestamp":"2026-06-01T14:39:05+00:00","service":"appointments-db","signal":"eligibility_query_duration_ms","value":2150},{"interpretation":"In trace_incident_02, the same pattern repeats: eligibility_query (s3) takes 2200ms of the 3320ms root duration (66.3% of total) and 88.7% of its parent check_eligibility span's 2480ms duration, confirming eligibility_query as the consistent critical-path bottleneck across degraded traces.","timestamp":"2026-06-01T14:52:31+00:00","service":"appointments-db","signal":"eligibility_query_duration_ms","value":2200},{"interpretation":"Error propagation in trace_incident_01 flows from deep to shallow: appointments-db eligibility_query (s3) fails with status=error, its parent payment-service check_eligibility (s2) also fails, and the root booking-service POST /book (s1) fails, consistent with a failure originating at the database query level and surfacing upward through the call chain.","timestamp":"2026-06-01T14:39:05+00:00","service":"appointments-db","signal":"status","value":"error"},{"interpretation":"The identical error-propagation pattern (db query error -> payment check_eligibility error -> booking root error) is confirmed in trace_incident_02, strengthening the inference that eligibility_query failures are the origin point rather than an artifact of a single trace.","timestamp":"2026-06-01T14:52:31+00:00","service":"appointments-db","signal":"status","value":"error"},{"interpretation":"Before/after contrast for eligibility_query shows a duration increase from 40ms (healthy, trace_pre_01) to 2150ms (trace_incident_01) and 2200ms (trace_incident_02) - roughly a 54-55x increase for the same operation, the largest relative degradation of any span observed.","timestamp":null,"service":"appointments-db","signal":"eligibility_query_duration_ms","value":"40 -> 2150/2200"},{"interpretation":"Before/after contrast for check_eligibility (payment-service) shows growth from 95ms healthy to 2410ms and 2480ms in the two incident traces - about a 25-26x increase, tracking closely with its child eligibility_query span, consistent with the payment-service span's duration being almost entirely explained by the downstream db call rather than payment-service's own processing.","timestamp":null,"service":"payment-service","signal":"check_eligibility_duration_ms","value":"95 -> 2410/2480"},{"interpretation":"reserve_slot (appointments-db) remains status=ok in both degraded traces despite the root request failing, and its share of the root duration stays small (310/3180=9.7% in trace_incident_01, 290/3320=8.7% in trace_incident_02), narrowing the fault away from the reserve_slot code path and toward eligibility_query/check_eligibility.","timestamp":null,"service":"appointments-db","signal":"reserve_slot_duration_ms","value":"38 -> 310/290 (status ok)"},{"interpretation":"Despite staying healthy (status=ok), reserve_slot's absolute duration still grew roughly 8x from baseline (38ms to 310ms/290ms) even though it is not a child of the failing check_eligibility/eligibility_query branch (parent=s1 in all traces), suggesting some shared resource contention or general service slowdown affecting even unaffected code paths, separate from the dominant eligibility_query bottleneck.","timestamp":null,"service":"appointments-db","signal":"reserve_slot_duration_ms","value":"38 -> 310/290"}],"gaps":["No trace spans exist below eligibility_query (e.g., internal db lock waits, connection pool waits, or downstream calls) to explain what specifically is consuming the 2150-2200ms inside that query.","No visibility into why reserve_slot, which is not a child of the failing check_eligibility/eligibility_query subtree, still slowed roughly 8x (38ms to ~290-310ms) in degraded traces - insufficient data to determine if this is shared resource contention, connection pool exhaustion, or an unrelated factor.","Only two degraded traces (trace_incident_01, trace_incident_02) and one healthy trace (trace_pre_01) are provided; no data on variance across a larger sample or on traces between 14:08:12 and 14:39:05 to pinpoint exactly when the eligibility_query degradation began within the incident window.","No spans for other services or endpoints are provided, so it cannot be determined whether the eligibility_query slowdown is isolated to the booking POST /book path or affects other operations using appointments-db."],"reasoning":"Compared span durations and status fields across the one pre-incident trace and two incident traces for the same POST /book operation. Computed each child span's duration as a fraction of its parent/root duration to identify the dominant contributor to the critical path (eligibility_query, consistently ~66-68% of root and ~89% of its parent). Traced status=error from the deepest failing span (eligibility_query) up through check_eligibility to the root POST /book to establish propagation direction. Contrasted absolute durations of matching operations between the healthy and degraded traces to quantify degradation magnitude. Identified reserve_slot as a span that stays functionally healthy (status=ok) and consumes a small root-duration fraction, narrowing the fault locus, while noting its duration still grew, which is flagged as a gap requiring further data."}
